questions.q: What is the time period of a simple pendulum?
questions.answer: The time period of a simple pendulum is given by T = 2π√(l/g) where l is the length of the pendulum and g is the acceleration due to gravity.

Simple harmonic motion is a type of periodic motion where the acceleration of the particle is directly proportional to the displacement from its mean position and is always directed towards the mean position.

questions.q: What is the displacement-time graph of a particle executing SHM?
questions.answer: The displacement-time graph of a particle executing SHM is a sinusoidal curve where the displacement varies sinusoidally with time.

questions.q: What is the equation of a simple pendulum?
questions.answer: The equation of a simple pendulum is θ = θ0 cos(ωt) where θ is the angular displacement, θ0 is the initial angular displacement, ω is the angular frequency and t is time.

questions.q: What is the energy of a particle executing SHM?
questions.answer: The energy of a particle executing SHM is given by E = (1/2)kA^2 where k is the spring constant and A is the amplitude.

questions.q: What is the velocity-time graph of a particle executing SHM?
questions.answer: The velocity-time graph of a particle executing SHM is a sinusoidal curve where the velocity varies sinusoidally with time.

questions.q: What is the relation between angular frequency and frequency?
questions.answer: The angular frequency (ω) is related to the frequency (f) by the equation ω = 2πf.

questions.q: What is the phase difference between two particles executing SHM?
questions.answer: The phase difference between two particles executing SHM is the difference in their phase angles.

questions.q: What is the amplitude of a particle executing SHM?
questions.answer: The amplitude of a particle executing SHM is the maximum displacement of the particle from its mean position.

questions.q: What is the mean position of a particle executing SHM?
questions.answer: The mean position of a particle executing SHM is the position where the particle comes to rest momentarily.
